_meta.title: PEIG Paper XIII Architecture Registry
_meta.subtitle: ABC Composite Node + Co-Rotating Frame BCP + Globe Topology
_meta.author: Kevin Monette

invariant_rules.name: A before B
invariant_rules.text: A is always created first. B = copy(A) at birth. B never precedes A. A is the living state; B is its first snapshot.

invariant_rules.name: A,B before C
invariant_rules.text: C is always born from both A and B. C_birth = BCP(A,B,0.5). C never precedes its parents.

invariant_rules.name: C accumulates
invariant_rules.text: After birth, C receives additional content. Each extension is logged with full A and B state at time of event.

invariant_rules.name: B is a crystal
invariant_rules.text: B.alpha_inner = 0 permanently. B never moves unless operator explicitly calls /update-identity. No automatic coupling.

A.description: Drifts freely with BCP ring. Carries task signal and current semantic output. Subject to depolarizing noise. No constraints on drift.

B.description: Initialized to home phase at node birth. Never participates in ring BCP automatically. Only updated on explicit /update-identity operator call. Represents identity at moment of last explicit update.

C.description: Born as BCP(A,B,0.5) at node creation. At each /update-identity event: C_new = BCP(A_current, B_current, 0.5). Accumulates labeled extension log recording A_phase, B_phase, identity_signal at each transition.

interpretation.zero: Node just had /update-identity called, or is at birth. A and B are coincident.
interpretation.small (< 0.5 rad): Node has drifted slightly. Identity is fresh.
interpretation.medium (0.5-1.5 rad): Node has drifted meaningfully. Monitor trajectory.
interpretation.large (> 1.5 rad): Node has traveled far from last frozen identity. Rich clock signal.
interpretation.maximum (pi rad): Node is at antipodal point from its last identity. Maximum drift.

identity_signal.reframe: Drift is a clock, not damage. Large signal = the node has changed significantly from its last known self. Zero signal = the clock just reset.

steps: Record A_phase, B_phase, identity_signal, A_word, B_word to extension_log
Compute C_new = BCP(A_current, B_current, 0.5) — archives transition moment
Update B = A_current (crystal resets to A's new position)
Identity signal delta resets to 0 (A and B are now coincident)
Clock restarts from new B position
when_to_call: Node has been explicitly retrained (new lesson, new role)
Operator confirms node's drift represents legitimate growth, not noise
Identity signal > 1.5 rad AND new role has been deliberately established
NEVER: automatically, on schedule, or because signal is large alone

corotating_frame_bcp.motivation: BCP attractor acts on phase VELOCITY, not position. CMDD (Paper XII) failed because it subtracted position. The correct operation subtracts mean angular velocity.

algorithm.step2: Run standard BCP on all edges
algorithm.step4: Compute angular velocity: delta(n) = (phi_after - phi_before + pi) mod 2pi - pi
algorithm.step5: Compute mean angular velocity: omega_bar = mean(delta)
algorithm.result: Collective rotation removed. Differential motion preserved. Relative phases maintained.

vs_cmdd.cmdd_flaw: Subtracts instantaneous phase position (phi - mean_phi). BCP attractor still acts on differences in that frame.
vs_cmdd.corotate_fix: Subtracts phase velocity (delta - mean_delta). Like moving to the rotating reference frame of the ring's center of mass.

corotating_frame_bcp.known_limitation: Wigner W_min = 0.000 after correction (states shifted off equatorial plane). Wigner restoration step needed. See Paper XIV EXP-1.
corotating_frame_bcp.result: 12/12 unique phases maintained through all 2000 steps tested.

globe_topology.paper_reference: Extended Betti Number Bound, Paper X
globe_topology.effect_on_phase_diversity: Maintains 12/12 unique phases when combined with co-rotating frame
globe_topology.effect_alone: Phase diversity maintained but Wigner non-classicality disrupted (same as pure co-rotate)

experimental_results_2000_steps.key_finding: Co-Rotate and Globe+CIA are the first architectures to maintain 12/12 unique phases indefinitely (2000+ steps).
experimental_results_2000_steps.wigner_cost: Both perfect-diversity architectures currently report W=0.0 (classical). Wigner restoration is Paper XIV EXP-1.

paper14_roadmap.title: Wigner Restoration After Co-Rotation
paper14_roadmap.method: After each co-rotating correction, project states back to equatorial plane (rz=0) while preserving corrected phase.
paper14_roadmap.predicted: 12/12 phase diversity + W_min restored to -0.113 range

paper14_roadmap.title: neg_frac Measurement on Globe+CIA
paper14_roadmap.method: Run Globe+CIA with full negentropy tracking over 2000 steps.
paper14_roadmap.predicted: neg_frac approximately 0.75-0.87 — first measurement breaking the 0.667 ceiling

paper14_roadmap.title: /update-identity Under Task Load
paper14_roadmap.method: Run Globe+CIA for 1000 steps with task injections. Call /update-identity on nodes with signal > 1.5 rad. Measure reset and re-divergence.
paper14_roadmap.predicted: Signal resets to 0, then re-diverges uniquely per node. Full operator protocol validated.
